title: Coda Integration Icon, Modal, and Settings Page
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to see a Coda export option alongside other integrations and easily access setup instructions, tutorials, and the Coda Pack installation link so that I can quickly start using the Coda integration.
description:
Add Coda as an export integration option in DataTools Pro with three components:

1. **Coda icon** - Add a Coda icon next to the Notion icon in the export toolbar
2. **Modal on click** - When the user clicks the Coda icon, display a modal containing:
   - An embedded YouTube tutorial video
   - A 'Get Coda Pack' button linking to the Coda pack listing
   - A 'Create Token for Coda' button
   - A 'View Documentation' button linking to the tutorial page
3. **Settings page** - Create a new /coda settings page with the same content and buttons as the modal, plus a placeholder for token management (to be wired up via REST API by another developer)

Resources:
- Coda Pack listing: https://coda.io/packs/datatools-pro-metrics-glossary-31833
- Tutorial video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=axKBfGYNdio
- Tutorial page: https://datatoolspro.com/tutorials/import-metrics-into-coda-with-datatools-pro-c/
acceptance_criteria: Coda icon appears next to the Notion icon in the export toolbar
Clicking the Coda icon opens a modal with video, pack link, token, and documentation buttons
New /coda settings page exists with the same content as the modal
Get Coda Pack button links to the correct Coda pack listing
View Documentation button links to the tutorial page
YouTube tutorial video is embedded in both modal and settings page
Token management section has a placeholder for future API token wiring
ux_changes: New Coda icon in the export toolbar next to Notion. Clicking opens a modal with embedded YouTube tutorial, Coda Pack installation link, token creation button, and documentation link. New /coda settings page with matching content and layout for token management.
implementation_notes: The Coda integration uses the DataTools Pro REST API rather than a direct database connection like Notion. The token management portion was wired up separately by another developer (MBT-872). Modal and settings page use a placeholder image layout initially.
comment_summary: Developer created the modal and settings page with placeholder content. Product owner provided the Coda pack listing URL, YouTube tutorial link, and documentation URL for integration.
